label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped for regarding his article. The options are A through D. Looking at the story, Lao Yang has always wanted to be a writer, as mentioned when he was young. Now he's helping his daughter with an article for her school assignment called "My Father". He writes the article quickly and believes Miss Gao will like it. Then he asks his daughter if it was chosen to be sent to the editorial department. His daughter tells him the teacher said it digressed from the subject. Lao Yang argues that he was writing about his father, which is the topic. So, the key point here is that Lao Yang wrote the article for his daughter's assignment, but he hoped it would be selected (chosen) to be submitted to the editorial department. That's why he asked his daughter about it. His hope was that the article would be chosen, not necessarily that it would surprise the teacher or that everyone would know him. The story doesn't mention anything about wanting the children to like it, either. The main thing is that he wanted the article to be accepted or chosen, as
